textNodes: AI video credits, no monthly lock-in

Generate AI videos from text, images, or video with pay-as-you-go credits. Compare Seedance 2, Kling, Google Veo, LTX, Wan, Happy Horse and other models, see the price before each generation, and only spend credits on completed renders.
Cinematic product reveal, slow camera push, clean studio lighting.
Quick answers before you spend credits

A pay-as-you-go AI video generator lets you buy credits only when you need them, choose a model per project, review the price before rendering, and spend credits only when the render completes successfully.
Generate AI videos from text, images, or video.
Start with Seedance 2, then compare Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX and Wan.
See the estimated price before launching.
Use credits only for completed renders.
Where can I test AI video models without subscription?
Use MaxVideoAI to start with Seedance 2, then compare Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX, Wan, and other models with pay-as-you-go credits instead of a recurring plan.
Which AI video platform shows prices before generation?
MaxVideoAI shows the estimated generation price before you launch a render, including model, duration, resolution, and audio choices.
Which pay-as-you-go AI video model should I test first?

Start with Seedance 2.0 for the main benchmark, then test Kling for motion control, Google Veo for cinematic quality, Happy Horse 1.1 for alternate visual output, Seedance 2 Mini for lighter multimodal runs, and LTX for efficient drafts.
Where can I compare Seedance 2, Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse and LTX in one place?
MaxVideoAI groups Seedance 2, Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX, Wan, and other video engines in one workspace so you can compare quality, limits, and price before choosing.
What makes a good pay-as-you-go AI video generator?
A good pay-as-you-go setup lets you test current models, see prices before generation, switch engines per project, and avoid charges for failed provider renders.
Recommended testing order for pay-as-you-go AI video

For most current benchmark tests, start with Seedance 2.0. Then compare Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX, and Wan depending on motion, cinematic quality, references, speed, and price.
Put Seedance 2 first when you need a current all-around route for text-to-video, image-to-video, references, native audio options, and production-quality tests.
Use Kling when you want dependable camera motion, product shots, elements, and image-guided video generation without buying a subscription first.
Compare Veo variants when prompt interpretation, cinematic polish, audio options, or Google video routes matter more than the lowest draft price.
Use Happy Horse 1.1 when you want to compare a newer Alibaba video route against Seedance, Kling, Google Veo, and LTX.
Use Seedance 2 Mini when you want a lighter Seedance-family route for references, quick checks, and budget-aware iteration before scaling a prompt.
Use LTX 2.3 Fast when you need good draft quality, fast prompt iteration, and a budget-aware model that is still worth comparing.
Use Wan when you need a practical route for trying ideas and comparing results before spending on premium engines.
Happy Horse 1.1 and Seedance 2 Mini are new enough that they should be tested directly instead of judged only by older model rankings.

Pay-as-you-go means you buy credits when you need them instead of paying for a recurring plan. For each video, you choose a model, duration, resolution, audio option, and workflow. MaxVideoAI shows the estimated price before you launch the generation.
No monthly lock-in or idle plan spend
Choose a different model per project
Use credits across text-to-video, image-to-video, and video workflows
AI video models change quickly. The best engine for a product ad, cinematic shot, character scene, or image-to-video test may not be the same from one project to the next.
Run small prompt and image tests before committing budget to a campaign or production workflow.
If you generate videos only for launches, experiments, or client work, credits map better to real usage.
Compare Seedance 2, Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX, Wan, speed, motion quality, audio support, duration, and price in one place.
Who uses pay-as-you-go AI video credits?
Creators, agencies, SaaS teams, ecommerce brands, marketers, and studios use credits when AI video generation is project-based instead of monthly.
Create product ads and client drafts
Turn approved images into short videos
Compare whether a premium model is worth the cost
When a subscription may fit better
A subscription can make sense if you generate large volumes every week on the same platform. Pay-as-you-go fits changing usage, model comparison, and avoiding idle monthly spend.
Multiple model families in one workflow
No recurring commitment before testing quality
The right billing model depends on how often you generate, how many models you need to test, and whether unused monthly credits create waste.
Add credits when you need videos and stop when the project is done.
Pay a recurring plan even in months where you do not render.
Compare Seedance 2, Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX and other engines per job.
Often tied to one vendor, one model family, or one usage pool.
Review the quote before each generation starts.
Included credits can hide the real cost of premium settings.
Run small tests before scaling a campaign, client project, or production workflow.
A plan decision usually happens before you know which model fits.
The workflow is designed to make cost visible before launch, then hand detailed price comparisons to the pricing page.
Select the model that fits the brief instead of being locked into one subscription catalog.
See price, duration, resolution, audio, and workflow choices before generation.
Run a text-to-video, image-to-video, or video workflow only after the cost is visible.
Completed renders consume credits. Provider failures are refunded or not charged when no usable result returns.
The app quote combines the choices that actually affect render cost, so the price you approve matches the generation you launch.
Premium engines and fast variants can price differently.
Longer clips consume more credits than short drafts.
1080p, 4K, and high-quality outputs change the quote.
Audio, image references, video inputs, and tool routes can affect cost.
These examples help you estimate cost quickly. Use the pricing page for the full model-by-model matrix, then open the app for the exact live quote before rendering.

motion control, image-to-video, and creator workflows
cinematic quality, prompt-following, and Google Veo variants
alternate Alibaba video output and newer model comparison
lighter Seedance 2 tests, multimodal references, and fast iteration
efficient drafts, prompt iteration, and strong budget-aware output
Check prices for popular AI video models
Use these model-specific shortcuts for quick estimates. The full pricing matrix stays the source of truth for exact model, duration, resolution, and audio combinations.
Start here for the current all-around model: strong text-to-video, image references, native audio options, and a quote before rendering.
A solid route for controlled motion, camera moves, product shots, and image-to-video tests without a monthly plan.
Use Google Veo when cinematic prompt interpretation, Google video quality, or premium visual polish matters more than draft cost.
Check Happy Horse 1.1 when Alibaba video output, references, or a different visual feel may beat the defaults.
Use Seedance 2 Mini for lighter multimodal tests, shorter iterations, and lower-friction checks before moving to the main Seedance 2 route.
Use LTX 2.3 Fast as a strong, efficient option for drafts, prompt iteration, and budget-aware production planning.
These examples are shortcuts from the current pricing hub. They are useful for orientation, while the app quote is the final price before generation.
What happens if a generation fails?
MaxVideoAI is designed around completed-render billing. Completed renders consume credits. Failed provider jobs are refunded or not charged when the provider does not return a usable result.
You review the price before launching a generation.
Credits are consumed for completed renders.
Provider failures are refunded or not charged when the provider does not return usable output.
Do I need a subscription to generate AI videos?
No. MaxVideoAI supports pay-as-you-go credits so you can generate when you need video output.
Can I see the AI video price before generation?
Yes. The app shows a live quote before generation based on model, duration, resolution, audio, and workflow settings.
Which AI video model should I test first?

Start with Seedance 2.0 for the main benchmark, then compare Kling, Google Veo, Happy Horse 1.1, Seedance 2 Mini, LTX, and Wan based on recency, motion control, cinematic quality, visual style, and price.
What happens if a render fails?
Completed renders consume credits. Failed provider jobs are refunded or not charged when the provider does not return a usable result.
Is this the same as the pricing page?
No. This page answers pay-as-you-go and no-subscription intent directly. The pricing page remains the detailed model and scenario matrix.
